name: Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau
name_original: Ascensión al Ori (Orhi) desde Puerto Larrau
description: Hike the difficult 2.7-mile (4.4 km) Ascension to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau for panoramic Pyrenean views and a significant climb.
region_intro: Hiking around Larrau offers access to the diverse landscapes of the French Pyrénées-Atlantiques. The region is characterized by rugged Pyrenean peaks, dramatic gorges carved by rivers, and extensive beech forests. Hikers can explore high mountain plateaus and pastoral lands, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Larrau The most popular hiking route is Holzarte footbridge, a 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route leads to the dramatic Holzarte footbridge, suspended high above the Olhadübi canyon. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau, a difficult 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail ascends Pic d'Orhy, the highest peak in the Basque Country, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenees. Local hikers also love the Escaliers peak, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Larrau is defined by Pyrenean peaks, deep gorges, and expansive beech forests. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Larrau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Larrau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Larrau?
A: Larrau offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with nearly 80 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Larrau?
A: Yes, Larrau has 17 easy routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. The Iraty Forest, Europe's largest beech forest, is an excellent location for family-friendly loops with numerous marked trails.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in the Larrau region?
A: For experienced hikers, Larrau offers 31 difficult routes. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1427006925/d-orhy-peak" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;D'Orhy peak&lt;/a&gt;, which is a demanding 18.2 km trail. Another challenging ascent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968755480/pic-d-orhy-trail-from-port-de-larrau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the highest peak in the Basque Country with panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular walks around Larrau?
A: Many trails in the Larrau region are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The Iraty Forest, for example, features numerous loops suitable for various levels. For a moderate circular option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e871374993/discovering-the-holzarte-footbridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Discovering the Holzarté footbridge&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Larrau?
A: Larrau is rich in natural wonders. You can visit the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/a&gt; suspended over the Olhadübi canyon, ascend &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt; for stunning Pyrenean views, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/876139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features extensive beech forests and high mountain plateaus.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or swimming spots accessible by hiking in Larrau?
A: Yes, the region offers natural swimming spots and waterfalls. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/876139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belabarzeko Urjausia - natural swimming pool&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable waterfall and a refreshing spot. The Pista Waterfall is another natural attraction in the area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Larrau?
A: The hiking experiences in Larrau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 460 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged Pyrenean peaks and dramatic gorges to the peaceful, extensive beech forests. The region's unique blend of natural beauty and cultural insights, such as pastoral landscapes and megalithic sites, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore on hikes in Larrau?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Larrau offers cultural insights. The Occabé plateau features an extraordinary megalithic site with well-preserved cromlechs (stone circles) dating back to 1000 BC. You'll also frequently encounter evidence of pastoralism, with sheep, cows, and horses grazing, and shepherds in 'cayolars' (mountain huts) during summer.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Larrau?
A: Larrau's landscape is incredibly varied. You'll encounter rugged Pyrenean peaks like Pic d'Orhy, deep gorges carved by rivers, and Europe's largest beech forest, Iraty. High mountain plateaus, pastoral lands with grazing animals, and limestone massifs also define the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Can I find lakes or reservoirs near hiking trails in Larrau?
A: Yes, there are several lakes and reservoirs in the vicinity of Larrau that can be incorporated into hiking plans. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3769664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Koixta Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2457928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irabia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and tranquil spots.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Larrau?
A: The best time to hike in Larrau is generally from spring through autumn, when the weather is milder and trails are more accessible. Summer offers warm temperatures ideal for high-altitude routes, while spring and autumn provide vibrant foliage and fewer crowds. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially for mountain ascents.
tips: The Pic d’Orhy is the highest point in the Basque Country at 2,017 meters above sea level. It offers an incredible panorama of the Bay of Biscay, the Pic d’Anie, Béarn and the first peaks of the Pyrenees. From Larrau, the ascent is along a sharp and arid ridge which remains accessible.

faqs.question: Where does the hike to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The hike typically begins at the parking area located at Puerto Larrau, which sits at an altitude of 1,573 meters. This is a convenient starting point, and parking is generally available there.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Ascension to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau?
faqs.answer: This ascent is generally considered to be of moderate to hard difficulty. While some parts are straightforward, the trail involves a continuous incline and can be steep, especially on the southern ridge. Good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau?
faqs.answer: The effective walking time to the summit is estimated at just over an hour. A total round trip, including the descent, usually takes about 2 hours and 15 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What are the main route options for ascending Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau?
faqs.answer: About 1.4 km from Puerto Larrau, the trail offers a bifurcation. You can choose between two main paths: one to the right for a steep and challenging climb along the southern ridge, or one to the left which is slightly longer but offers a more gradual and less steep gradient, flanking the mountain.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the summit of Ori (Orhi)?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Pic d'Orhy, you can enjoy expansive panoramic views. These include the impressive Irati Forest, the Salazar Valley, and other significant Pyrenean peaks like Pic d'Anie and Midi d'Ossau. On clear days, views can even extend to the Cantabrian Sea.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is generally during the summer months when conditions are most favorable. Winter and spring can be significantly more challenging due to snow accumulation and icy ridges, requiring proper equipment and experience.

faqs.question: Is the Ascension to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, many trails in the Pyrenees are dog-friendly. However, given the moderate to hard difficulty and potentially steep sections, ensure your dog is fit for the ascent. Always keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of local wildlife and other hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ascension to Ori (Orhi) from Puerto Larrau. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You'll find it covers parts of the Étape 06, Euskal Herriko ibilaldia, and [CIMA NA01] Larrau trails.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Ori (Orhi)?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. The initial part involves a continuous incline through gentle, green slopes. As you ascend, you'll encounter more challenging sections, including potentially steep and rocky paths, especially if you choose the harder ascent route. The descent can also be steep and pronounced in places.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or features along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning panoramic views from Pic d'Orhy, you'll pass by hunter's cabins at the start of the trail. Ori (Orhi) is also notable as the westernmost 2,000-meter peak in the Pyrenees, offering a unique geographical distinction.
